feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Integration Suite
feed.subtitle: SAP Integration Suite Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: [CPI-Groovy]: Sort XML Nodes based on the start date
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have a requirement to sort the EmpJob nodes based on start dates only when there is sinlge EmpEmployment node and jobInfoNav has multiple EmpJob nodes which contains emplStatusNav/PicklistOption/localeLabel is Active in the ascending order. Eg. I am expecting EmpJob/startDate = 2024-11-01T00:00:00.000 in the first node and then EmpJob/startDate = 2025-01-01T00:00:00.000 in the second node. The payload contains multiple records, if these conditions are not satisfied there should not be any sorting done to nodes.

I have tried with the below-mentioned code but it is not working.

Any help or suggestions would be highly appreciated.

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;
import java.util.HashMap;
import groovy.xml.XmlUtil;
import groovy.util.NodeList
import groovy.util.XmlParser;
import groovy.xml.MarkupBuilder;
import java.util.Date;
import java.text.SimpleDateFormat;
import java.util.Calendar;

def Message processData(Message message) {
    def body = message.getBody(java.lang.String);
    def payload = new XmlParser().parseText(body);
    
        payload.PerPerson.each{rec ->  
        def countofEmpEmployment = rec.employmentNav.EmpEmployment.size()
        if (countofEmpEmployment <= 1){
            def countofEmpJob = rec.employmentNav.EmpEmployment.jobInfoNav.EmpJob.size();
            def EmpJobStatus = rec.employmentNav.EmpEmployment.jobInfoNav.EmpJob.emplStatusNav.PicklistOption.localeLabel.text()
            if(EmpJobStatus.contains("ActiveActive")){
            rec.value = rec.employmentNav.EmpEmployment.jobInfoNav.EmpJob.sort{sd ->
                    sd.startDate.text()}
            }
        }
    } 
    message.setBody(XmlUtil.serialize(payload));
    return message;
}

Regards,
Pavan

entry.title: Combining multiple IDOCs into one file at the receiving end in SAP CPI.
entry.content:
Hi, 

I have a requirement to combine multiple idocs into one single file. It is a DESADV.DELVRY03 structure. 

So the output structure should be like;

<DELVRY03>

<IDOC Begin="1">

</IDOC>

</DELVRY03>

For my requirement, it uses Trading Partner Management and in TPM only packet size of 1 supports. So before entering TPM I have used a splitter, and at the receiver end, I have used a custom integration flow and I need to combine the IDOCs using SAP_CorrelationID. 

How is it possible to do the above requirement in SAP CPI?

Thanks,

Radhika

entry.title: Streamlining Business Processes with B2B Integration Factory
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced business world, effective communication and efficient data exchange between business partners are crucial. The B2B Integration Factory, a powerful solution designed to simplify and streamline B2B interactions, can transform your business processes. 

In one of our recent projects, I delved deep into the possibilities of the B2B Integration Factory and discovered some insights. At first, it can feel like a lot to take in. But once you get the hang of it, you'll see how useful it can be.  

What is B2B Integration Factory? 

The B2B Integration Factory is a service within SAP that enables organizations to manage their B2B processes more efficiently.

The Partner Directory offers centralized partner management, enabling you to store and manage all partner-specific configurations in one place. This ensures consistency and simplifies the maintenance of partner data. 

Additionally, the B2B Integration Factory provides a wide range of predefined integration content, including mappings, schemas, and connectivity options, which significantly reduce implementation time and effort. 

It is built to handle high transaction volumes and can effortlessly scale to meet your business's growing needs. It supports a variety of sender and receiver communication protocols, including AS2, SFTP, HTTP, and also custom options. 

Last but not least, the B2B Integration Factory ensures secure data exchange with features like encryption, digital signatures, and compliance with industry standards such as EDIFACT, X12, TRADACOMS and more 

 Conclusion 

The B2B Integration Factory in SAP makes B2B interactions easier and more efficient. It cuts down on manual work, reduces mistakes, and speeds up transactions. By using ready-made content, it saves on setup and maintenance costs. Plus, with real-time monitoring and analytics, businesses can keep track of data exchanges and stay competitive in today’s connected world. 

If you would like to learn more, please Contact Proclus - www.proclus-group.com

entry.title: SAP BTP Cockpit free trail account - error "Service" broker
entry.content:
I am encountering an issue when attempting to execute the boost, which consistently fails with the following error message:

Service broker error: "Service" broker apimanagement-apiportal-trial failed with: "Forbidden"

In addition to this, I have attempted the following troubleshooting steps:

  1. Manually creating an instance of the service.
  2. Re-creating the sub-account associated with the service.

Unfortunately, neither of these approaches resolved the issue, and the error persists.

Does anyone happen to know what it could be?

entry.title: SAP API Management - Set Basic Authentication for API Proxy in Integration Suite
entry.content:
Dear All,

In SAP Integration Suite, Basic Authentication offers a simple way to safeguard your API proxies using a username and password. This blog covers how to configure Basic Authentication for your API proxy in API Management.

Follow the Below Steps to Set Basic Authentication to your API Proxy.

Step 1 : Create a Proxy with your target endpoint (For test purpose i used httpbin URL)

https://httpbin.org/get

 

 



Step 2 : Go to Policies and Assign Basic Authentication in PreFlow Incoming request Stream

 

 Provide the below code in the policy editor:

 

<BasicAuthentication async='true' continueOnError='false' enabled='true' xmlns='http://www.sap.com/apimgmt'>
	<Operation>Decode</Operation>
	<IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>true</IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>
	<User ref='current.username'></User>
	<Password ref='current.password'></Password>
	<Source>request.header.Authorization</Source>
</BasicAuthentication>

 

 

Step 3 : Add Raise Fault Policy in PreFlow Incoming Stream (Next to the Basic Authentication Policy)

 

 

<RaiseFault async="true" continueOnError="false" enabled="true" xmlns="http://www.sap.com/apimgmt">
    <FaultResponse>
        <Set>
            <Headers/>
            <Payload contentType="application/json">{"status" : "Error", "messege" : "401 Unauthorized" } </Payload>
            <StatusCode>401</StatusCode>
            <ReasonPhrase>Unauthorized</ReasonPhrase>
        </Set>
    </FaultResponse>
    <IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>true</IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>
</RaiseFault>

 

In RaiseFault Policy ConditionString Provide below Code (Replace "YOUR_USERNAME" & "YOUR_PASSWORD" with your credentials)

 

(current.username != "YOUR_USERNAME")  OR (current.password != "YOUR_PASSWORD")

 

Save and Deploy.

Testing : 

To Test the Basic Authentication Try a GET Call for Your API Proxy in PostMan:

Positive Testing : 

Provide the exact Username and Password given in the Condition String :


Negative Testing : 

Provide an incorrect username and password to verify if access is correctly denied.

Incorrect Username :

Incorrect Password :

Thank You!

entry.title: Form data option not working in SAP CPI but working in POSTMAN - 404 Error
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have created a flow to extract the base 64 encoded file from a field called <FileContent> and decoded the same and send to JIRA in form-data format. I have added the relevent details in the header but it is failing by 404 error in SAP CPI.

Tried taking help from the below mentioned blog, but still it is not working.

Strange is that when I am sending the same file(decoded one as well) from POSTMAN it is working and delivering fine but through SAP CPI it is failing with below error.

Error Details
org.apache.camel.component.ahc.AhcOperationFailedException: HTTP operation failed invoking https://bseXXXtlassian.net/rest/api/3/issue/78053/attachments with statusCode: 404
 
Please suggest, if I am missing any configuration here?
Thanks,
Nidhi Srivastava

entry.title: Is there any Predefined Integration Content for data replication between S4 HANA Cloud and S4 HANA?
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

 

I am now discovering a wide variety of predefined integration content that I can use in Integration Suite.

https://api.sap.com/discoverintegrations

I am wondering if there is any integration scenario between S4 HANA Cloud and S4 HANA? For example, data replication of cost center between the 2 systems.

I can not find any...(those which were shown on the list after choosing S4 HANA Cloud and S4 HANA do not seem like what I expect.)

Or the fact that there's none of them is because usually this kind of integration system will not require to use integration suite, but other sap products?

Thank you.

entry.title: How to read xml attached to a PDF file in SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have a requirement to read xml attached to a PDF file. Please let me know if same can be achieved somehow in SAP CPI.

Thanks in Advance!

Mayank

entry.title: Resolving Corrupted ZIP Files in SAP CPI During Ariba ITK Integration
entry.content:
Issue: 
In SAP CPI, when retrieving procurement information from Ariba using ITK, large data volumes cause the ZIP file in the response to become corrupted. This corruption prevents the ZIP Splitter from extracting and processing the file.

Root Cause:
When the data volume in Ariba is large, Ariba sends null bytes in the response ZIP file to keep the connection alive. These null bytes precede the actual ZIP content, causing the file to appear corrupted.

Upon investigation, we noticed that a valid ZIP file should start with the header 50 4B (the standard ZIP signature). However, in the corrupted files, null bytes (00) were added before this header, rendering the file invalid.

Investigation Details:

  • Exported ZIP files from Ariba via ITK were inspected using a Hex Editor (e.g., Notepad++ with a HEX plugin).
  • The corrupted file had a significant number of null bytes (00) before the valid ZIP header (50 4B or similar).
  • These null bytes are intentional and part of Ariba's mechanism to keep the connection alive during long-running downloads.

Solution:
To resolve this issue, a Groovy script was developed to remove the null bytes from the ZIP file before processing. This script ensures the file integrity is restored by verifying and cleaning the ZIP header.

Here is the Groovy script:

 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import java.util.zip.ZipInputStream
import java.util.zip.ZipOutputStream
import java.util.zip.ZipEntry
import java.io.ByteArrayInputStream
import java.io.ByteArrayOutputStream

def Message processData(Message message) {
    // Get the ZIP file from the message body as a byte array
    byte[] zipBytes = message.getBody(byte[])

    // Remove null bytes only before the first occurrence of 0x50 0x4B
    byte[] cleanBytes = removeNullBytesBeforeSignature(zipBytes)

    // Repackage the cleaned bytes into a valid ZIP file containing all files
    byte[] reZippedBytes = repackageZip(cleanBytes)

    // Add the re-zipped file as an attachment in the message log (optional)
    def messageLog = messageLogFactory.getMessageLog(message)
    if (messageLog != null) {
        messageLog.addAttachmentAsString("ReZipped_File", new String(reZippedBytes, "ISO-8859-1"), "application/zip")
    }

    // Return the re-zipped bytes as a new InputStream to the message body
    message.setBody(new ByteArrayInputStream(reZippedBytes))
    return message
}

// Function to remove null bytes only before the first occurrence of 0x50 0x4B
byte[] removeNullBytesBeforeSignature(byte[] fileBytes) {
    int indexOfPK = findSignatureIndex(fileBytes, [0x50, 0x4B] as byte[])
    if (indexOfPK == -1) {
        // If the signature is not found, return the original array
        return fileBytes
    }
    // Separate the data before and after the signature
    byte[] beforeSignature = fileBytes[0..<(indexOfPK)]
    byte[] afterSignature = fileBytes[indexOfPK..<fileBytes.length]

    // Remove all null bytes from the section before the signature
    beforeSignature = beforeSignature.findAll { it != 0x00 } as byte[]

    // Combine the cleaned part with the rest of the data
    byte[] result = new byte[beforeSignature.length + afterSignature.length]
    System.arraycopy(beforeSignature, 0, result, 0, beforeSignature.length)
    System.arraycopy(afterSignature, 0, result, beforeSignature.length, afterSignature.length)

    return result
}

// Function to find the index of the first occurrence of a byte signature
int findSignatureIndex(byte[] data, byte[] signature) {
    for (int i = 0; i <= data.length - signature.length; i++) {
        boolean match = true
        for (int j = 0; j < signature.length; j++) {
            if (data[i + j] != signature[j]) {
                match = false
                break
            }
        }
        if (match) {
            return i
        }
    }
    return -1
}

// Function to repackage cleaned bytes into a valid ZIP containing all original files
byte[] repackageZip(byte[] cleanedData) {
    ByteArrayOutputStream byteArrayOutputStream = new ByteArrayOutputStream()
    ZipOutputStream zipOutputStream = new ZipOutputStream(byteArrayOutputStream)

    // Read the cleaned bytes as a ZIP file
    ZipInputStream zipInputStream = new ZipInputStream(new ByteArrayInputStream(cleanedData))

    ZipEntry entry
    while ((entry = zipInputStream.getNextEntry()) != null) {
        // Copy each entry to the new ZIP file
        zipOutputStream.putNextEntry(new ZipEntry(entry.getName()))
        byte[] buffer = new byte[1024]
        int bytesRead
        while ((bytesRead = zipInputStream.read(buffer)) != -1) {
            zipOutputStream.write(buffer, 0, bytesRead)
        }
        zipOutputStream.closeEntry()
        zipInputStream.closeEntry()
    }

    zipInputStream.close()
    zipOutputStream.close()

    return byteArrayOutputStream.toByteArray()
}

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite Custom UDF in Message Mapping
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I need help creating a Groovy UDF to split a property value (containing a comma-separated string) into multiple values within the same context in SAP PI/PO message mapping.

For example, the property value:
item=000010,000030,000020

I want to extract each value (000010, 000030, 000020) as separate outputs in the same context.

Here’s the Groovy script I wrote:

def void getProperty(String propertyName, MappingContext context, Output output) {
def propertyValue = context.getProperty(propertyName)
if (propertyValue) {
def values = propertyValue.split(",")
values.each { value ->
output.addValue(value.trim())
}
} else {
output.addValue("")
}
}

However, the function doesn’t appear in the "Custom UDF" section. Can someone help identify what I might be doing wrong or suggest improvements to achieve this functionality?

Thank you!

entry.title: SAP Cloud Integration Error: HTTP header is larger than 8192 bytes.
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

When processing a large payload in Cloud Integration with a S/4HANA Cloud target system using HTTP Adapter, I got the following error:

io.netty.handler.codec.TooLongFrameException: HTTP header is larger than 8192 bytes

 

I followed SAP Note 3146809(https://me.sap.com/notes/3146809/E) and tried the resolution(set "Cookie|x-csrf-token as Request Headers) , but the error still remained.

(P.s. I have also checked SAP Note 3537811 (https://me.sap.com/notes/3537811/E) but it does not really help because it merely asks me to reduce the size of HTTP header getting returned without letting me know how)

Does anyone know how to fix this?

===================================

As your reference, the following screenshots show the setting in my integration flow, which relate to http headers.

entry.title: CPI - Adapter RabbitMQ consumer Message - Wrong converstion to base64
entry.content:
I am using SAP CPI with a Groovy script, and when I post this message to the queue, it appears as Base64. However, in the logs of my middleware (SAP CPI), it is shown as a regular message.

When I post it to an online webhook, it also arrives normally as JSON.

Only in Adapter(AMQP)RabbitMQ does it arrive in Base64. The version is 4.0, and AMQP 1.0 is being used.

 

Message encode64 post in rabbit

eyJwYXR0ZXJuIjoidGVzdC5ldnQiLCJkYXRhIjp7IklEIjoiNjYzIiwiS2V5U0FQIjoiMDAwMSIsIlN0YXR1cyI6IlMiLCJNZW5zYWdlU0FQIjoiQ2xpZW50ZSAwMDAxIGNyZWF0ZWQgd2l0aCBzdWNjZXNzLiJ9fQ== 

Coding Groovy:

 

 

 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message

Message processData(Message message) { 
def fixedBody = '{"pattern":"test.evt","data":{"ID":"663","KeySAP":"0001","Status":"S","MensageSAP":"Cliente 0001 created with success."}}'

message.setBody(fixedBody)


message.setHeader("Content-Type", "text/plain")
message.setHeader("CamelHttpCharacterEncoding", "UTF-8")

return message 
}

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite: Transporting Script Collection
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts,

     I have a question regarding usage of Script Collections in SAP Integration Suite.

Context: In my case, script collection contains the common scripts being utilized by multiple iFlows. 

Issue: There are few Groovy scripts in the script collection which are complete and tested. However, there are few scripts which are currently being worked upon or not yet fully tested. Now I want to transport the scripts to QA/Prod systems which are complete. However, the transport option is only available at the Script Collection Object Level but not at individual script level which means transporting the whole script collection will transport the incomplete/untested scripts as well. How can this be avoided?

#SAPIntegrationSuite, #SAPiFlow #SAPCPI

Thanks and Regards,

    Abhijeet

entry.title: How to handle constantly changing host key of SFTP server
entry.content:
Hello, 
I've a requirement of maintaining the known host file of an SFTP server, but each time I'm trying to obtain the host key fingerprint in the connectivity test in BTP monitoring, I'm getting a different host key every time and it is proving to be hard to maintain it in the known host file. 

when I tried to communicate to the owner of the SFTP server, they said they have set it in a way that it'll change dynamically each time we try to obtain the key. Which is why I'm getting an error while connecting to the server afterwards.

any ideas about how to go about this?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Push all individual records in a single mail
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I am getting response from the receiver system as individual records(one by one), Now I just want to send all the records at a time in a single mail.

I know we can achieve this using gather option, but I want to achieve this using data store operation.

I just tried with write and select data store operations but select operation is triggering multiple times whenever the record is coming but I want it to trigger after getting all records.

Could anyone help me on this?

Thanks,

Pavan Koppula

entry.title: XML Namespace Error with XPATH condition
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We have below as the content which needs to be routed based on the field 'Message' value. 

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<error xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices/metadata">
  <code>06/019</code>
  <message xml:lang="en">Document  does not exist</message>
  <innererror>
    <application>
      <component_id/>
      <service_namespace>/SAP/</service_namespace>
      <service_id>TEST</service_id>
      <service_version>0001</service_version>
    </application>
    <transactionid>d828d56baed54a718432432e7cd27533</transactionid>
    <timestamp/>
    <Error_Resolution>
      <SAP_Transaction/>
      <SAP_Note>See SAP Note 1797736 for error analysis (https://service.sap.com/sap/support/notes/1797736)</SAP_Note>
    </Error_Resolution>
    <errordetails>
      <errordetail>
        <ContentID/>
        <code>06/033</code>
        <message>Document  does not exist</message>
        <propertyref/>
        <severity>error</severity>
        <target/>
        <transition>false</transition>
      </errordetail>
    </errordetails>
  </innererror>
</error>

 

Tried the condition = //innererror/errordetails/errordetail[contains(message, 'Document')]

Somehow, it is not working due to the namespace. I've also added the namespace in the runtime configuration as xmlns=http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices/metadata Still getting invalid namespace declared error. 

Appreciate your valuable inputs and support. 

Thanks,

Barath Vivekanandan

entry.title: SAP ECC to BTP Integration Suite Proxy connection
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have the following situation where sender SAP ECC system would like to send XML proxy message to BTP Integration Suite.. whereas we also have SAP PO in our landscape.. SAP ECC ABAP developer would like to get our SAP PO ESR objects reflected on their SAP ECC system.. But, in runtime the messages should flow via BTP Integration Suite.. How to acheive this , should we ask BASIS guys to create two G type destinations on SAP ECC like mentioned below:

1.  "G" type destination(SAP_PROXY_ESR) to connect to SAP PO ESR. With the help of this destination, ESR objects should be visible in SAP ECC (SPROXY).

2. Another "G" type destination to route the messages to BTP Integration Suite.

Could you please guide me to acheive the above requirement?

Please let me know if something important is missed.

 

Thanks,

Ramu.

entry.content:

Thanks，

Sean

1.My integration flow ：

2.Set Header：

3.RabbitMQ Set

4.Test ：My Custome Headr [url] not send to mq

entry.title: No run steps visible in sap cpi trace
entry.content:
Hi ,

No run steps visible in sap cpi trace.

what could be the reason plz suggest.

entry.title: Messages struck in event mesh
entry.content:
Hello Friends,

I'm not able to understand the reason for messages getting struck in the event mesh and why is CPI not able to poll the messages successfully. This happens every now and then. below is the configuration of AMQP adapter.

How to fix this?
